pattern: A running prefix sum lets you compare each side of a split in constant time, so one left-to-right sweep counts every valid cut.
hints: The two sides of a split share a fixed total. If you know the sum of the left part, the right part is determined.
Precompute the total, then walk i from left to right maintaining the left-side sum so far. The right side is total minus left.
Let total be the full sum. For each i from 0 to n-2, add nums[i] to a running left sum and increment the answer when left >= total - left.

edge_inputs.description: negatives shift where splits become valid
trailing zeros keep later splits valid
all maximum values, every split valid except where equal
equality counts as valid since the test is >=
all zeros, every split is an equality

stress.description: large array of mixed-sign values to exercise the prefix-sum sweep
stress.note: wide value range with negatives so valid splits scatter unpredictably
